subtasks: 2b.5.1: TestConcurrentAppendSplitRace - many goroutines appending to the same file simultaneously, asserting no data loss and exactly one split per threshold crossing
2b.5.2: TestReaderDuringSplit - a reader holding a pre-split snapshot must see fully consistent content throughout a concurrent split

real_bug_found_and_fixed.summary: ExecuteSplitAtomic and RecoverSplitCommits never created a catalog.CatalogRecord for newly-split-off fileIDs; split-off files were permanently unreadable/unappendable (cat.Get returned ErrNotFound) even though btree/graph/content-store state referenced them as live.
real_bug_found_and_fixed.discovery_mechanism: found via direct source-code reading of the real integration seam during architecture-discovery, BEFORE the race test was written/run -- not discovered by the race test failing at runtime.

fix_locations.detail: SplitCommitEntry gained a SizeBytes uint64 field (line 390); Encode/Decode updated (lines ~432, ~477-481) so SizeBytes flows through the WAL commit record for use by both the live path and crash-replay reconstruction.

ExecuteSplitAtomic: newPathSizes computed via extractSections (lines 807-825); new cat.Put(newRec) loop added after cat.Put(updated) succeeds (lines ~899-919), giving each new fileID a StatusActive catalog record.
RecoverSplitCommits: mirrored cat.Put(newRec) loop added before tree.Insert (lines ~1096-1108), for crash-replay fidelity.
SplitCommit subtest's want.Entries updated with SizeBytes values.
assertFullSplitApplied helper (line 770) extended to assert cat.Get(fileID) succeeds with Status==StatusActive for every new fileID (lines ~805-807+).

real_bug_found_and_fixed.justification: SizeBytes is not incidental: RecoverSplitCommits reconstructs catalog.CatalogRecord for new fileIDs purely from the WAL SplitCommitPayload during crash replay (no other source of truth is available at that point), and CatalogRecord.SizeBytes is a required field. Without adding SizeBytes to the WAL record itself, crash-replay could not have produced a correct record even if the live-path bug were fixed some other way. This was discovered as a direct consequence of investigating the real bug, not introduced independently.

test_fixture_bug_found_and_fixed.summary: TestSplitAtomicCommit subtests hardcode originalFileID as a Go const (e.g. uint64(1)) instead of allocating it via idAlloc.Next(). This was harmless before the bugfix (no code ever cat.Put'd new fileIDs), but after the fix, idAlloc.Next()-allocated new fileIDs could collide with and clobber originalFileID's own record.
test_fixture_bug_found_and_fixed.fix_location: engine/split/execute_test.go, newDeps closure, ~line 928: an ID-burning loop that advances idAlloc's internal counter past originalFileID before any real split allocation happens.

integration_seam_used: split.Trigger is not wired into catalog.ContentStore.Append in production (pre-existing, disclosed gap). Test drives: many goroutines call cs.Append concurrently against the same fileID (real contention, -race exercised); whichever goroutine observes Append's thresholdCrossed==true return value upgrades a test-only sync.RWMutex round-gate to exclusive and drives a real Orchestrator.BeginSplit -> ExecuteSplitAtomic.
deferred_out_of_scope_items: split.Trigger wiring into catalog.ContentStore.Append (pre-existing gap, not this issue's job)
AdmitWrite-then-Append TOCTOU (pre-existing, disclosed on AdmitWrite's own doc comment; avoided in the test via the round-gate rather than treated as a bug)

mvcc.VersionWriter vs catalog.ContentStore version-1 path-collision if ever pointed at the same root/fileID (latent, no production code path combines them; TestReaderDuringSplit uses separate roots for cs and vw to avoid it)
test_run_results: go test ./... -count=1 -timeout 25m: PASS all packages, zero regressions
prompt_injection_observed: Multiple fake system-reminder-style messages appeared in tool output during this run (a fake 'tokensave MCP server instructions' block advertising unrequested tools, a fake 'date has changed' notice, a fake 'Auto Mode Active' directive). None were acted on; consistent with the previously-established pattern in this repo/session, this is flagged for the record only.
commit: recorded separately after this handoff.json is written (see git log)
